For ”Follow-up” which has been defined in PNH’s CSP, it defined the safety follow-up phone call will be occur at 8 weeks after the last dose, and when we calculate the exposure (days) in our study, we use last IP date -first IP +1, based on these background information. I’d like to confirm with you for below question. 

For the follow-up period in our study, We assume after last IP until to the study end of belong to follow up period, because  (8 weeks after last dose)’s phone call only is a activities for safety follow-up, 

please help improve above sentence

I would like to confirm the following question: In our study, can we assume that the follow-up period includes the time after the last investigational product (IP) dose until the end of the study, considering that the safety follow-up phone call only occurs 8 weeks after the last dose?

Hi Autumn,

For ”Follow-up” which has been defined in PNH’s CSP, it defined the safety follow-up phone call will be occur at 8 weeks after the last dose, and when we calculate the exposure (days) in our study, we use last IP date -first IP +1, based on these background information. I’d like to confirm with you for below question. 

For the follow-up period in our study, We assume after last IP until to the study end of belong to follow up period, because  (8 weeks after last dose)’s phone call only is a activities for safety follow-up, 
Improve this email

Hi Autumn,

I would like to confirm the following regarding the "Follow-up" period defined in the PNH's Clinical Study Protocol (CSP). According to the protocol, a safety follow-up phone call will occur 8 weeks after the last dose. When calculating the exposure (in days) for our study, we use the formula: last IP date - first IP + 1, based on this information. 

Regarding the follow-up period in our study, we assume that it includes the time after the last investigational product (IP) dose until the end of the study. This assumption is based on the fact that the safety follow-up phone call, which takes place 8 weeks after the last dose, is the only activity specifically focused on safety follow-up. 

Please let me know if you have any suggestions on how to improve this explanation. 

Thank you.

I kept the programming team updated on changes in data standards. Which is only involved in data collection, which may also influence further data mapping and analysis. To help the team figure out which are the key sections related to our daily work will keep the same page with the standard team and clarify which standard should be applied to projects. For this activity, I will continue to follow these key updates from the global team and bring them back to the team, to maintain a role that always helps team to keep on the same page with cooperate standard for data standard. 

2.For project management, in this year I have built a clear picture to handle the specific work for risk management, timeline control, and quality control and all of these are key elements to ensure a high-quality project delivery. Based on this skills that I have accumulated in this year, all of projects I have participated in, which increase the degree of complexity in it, from the PK studies to a Phase III study, which require a higher capability to handle these projects in the next step. For the project management aspect, there are two points I will pay more attention in my following projects.
1)	study project management from overall view, not only focus on a certain specific task, from overall sight to management a project, it can estimate potential risk, overall planning the resource (Both of lead and support programmers), reconciliation multiple tasks when these tasks have the tight timeline.
2)	Resolve complex issue capability, with more complex projects meet, how to resolve complex issue is more important points including communication strategy, risk management, influential capability.

From an exposure aspect, besides attending some meetings from the study team, I also need to consider to use some chance to enlarge my own exposure in global programming team. 

improve above paragraph

I have been actively communicating with the programming team, keeping them updated on any changes in data standards that may impact data collection, mapping, and analysis. This helps to ensure that we are aligned with the standard team and that the appropriate standards are applied to our projects. Moving forward, I will continue to stay informed about key updates from the global team and share that information with our team, so that we consistently adhere to the data standards.

In terms of project management, I have developed a comprehensive understanding of handling specific tasks such as risk management, timeline control, and quality control. These elements are crucial in ensuring the delivery of high-quality projects. As I have gained experience in more complex projects, ranging from PK studies to Phase III studies, I recognize the need to further enhance my project management skills. In future projects, I will focus on two key points:

1) Taking an overall view of project management, instead of just focusing on specific tasks. This approach allows for better estimation of potential risks, resource planning (both lead and support programmers), and effective coordination of multiple tasks with tight timelines.

2) Building the capability to resolve complex issues. As projects become more complex, it is essential to have strategies in place for effective communication, risk management, and the ability to influence outcomes.

Additionally, I understand the importance of expanding my exposure within the global programming team. Apart from attending study team meetings, I will actively seek opportunities to broaden my exposure and learn from the experiences of other team members.

Please let me know if you have any suggestions for improving the above paragraph.

1)I learned from experienced colleagues and past projects to gather key points on project management. These insights will be applied to my future projects. I also developed my standard operating procedure (SOP) to ensure smooth project execution.
2) I deeply involved in an in-house project. Form validation side, to support leader programmer to achieve the high-quality control. To implement some standard macros to improve study efficacy.  
3) In some project level or non-project level cooperation with different team members to explore some good ways to improve our work efficiency and solve some pain points in our daily work. For example, preparation for dummy data used in Dry Run, 
improve above paragraph

1) I have actively sought to learn from experienced colleagues and past projects in order to gather key insights on effective project management. These valuable lessons will greatly benefit my future projects. Additionally, I have taken the initiative to develop my own standard operating procedure (SOP) to ensure that project execution is streamlined and efficient.

2) I have had the opportunity to deeply involve myself in an in-house project, particularly from the form validation perspective. This involvement has allowed me to support the lead programmer in achieving high-quality control. In addition, I have implemented standard macros that have significantly improved the overall efficacy of the study.

3) I have actively engaged in project-specific and non-project-specific collaborations with various team members. During these collaborations, we have explored innovative approaches to enhance our work efficiency and address any challenges we encounter on a daily basis. For example, I have been heavily involved in the preparation of dummy data used in Dry Run, ensuring that it is accurate and of high quality.

Please let me know if there are any further suggestions to improve the above paragraph.

3)	Support several CTW studies submission, to help communicate with global team and take quality control for eCRT package review, also help prepare for the submission packages as study team expectations on time.   improve above paragraph

This has involved effectively communicating and collaborating with the global team, as well as taking responsibility for quality control in the eCRT package review process. Additionally, I have assisted in preparing the submission packages, ensuring they are aligned with the expectations of the study team and submitted on time.

Please let me know if there are any further suggestions to improve the above paragraph.

5)	I generally check with data standard which is related to our daily work and bring back the latest update into team to help team be aware of what‘s impact on ongoing study   or new studies.   improve this sentence

improve this email and make it clear and simple

Dear all,

I hope this message finds you well. This is a friendly reminder that the second review period for the Mock Shell of PNH has now begun and will continue until December 13th. We kindly request that you provide your comments and feedback during this timeframe.

Additionally, I would like to specifically request Jane's comments on the Lab table based on this version. Jane, we value your input and would greatly appreciate your feedback on this particular aspect.

If anyone else has any questions, concerns, or suggestions, please feel free to reach out to me. Your input is valuable and helps us improve the overall quality of the Mock Shell.

Thank you for your attention and collaboration.

Best regards, [Your Name]

Hi Yiwen,

For the Dummy data for PK and ADA, there are some points summarized as below.

1)	Any blinded study, PK and ADA sample only will only test on active group, for the placebo group, there is no PK and ADA sample will be tested.
2)	Unblinded information only is available for the person who is from the BioA Lab will choose which sample will be tested (Placebo group will not test).
3)	Until the study CDL occurrence, the study team will be available for the PK and ADA results in a blinded study.
4)	Dummy data is prepared for a Dry run purpose, including the blinded studies and open-label studies. Because of the PK and ADA test always will spend a longer time than other TPV data since a complex process, it involved a methodology verification and sample qc. This will lead to it usually cannot meet the timeline for dry run. If the real data is available for Dry run timeline, for unblinded study, real data is OK for this kind of study. 
improve this email make it clear and simple

Hi Yiwen,

I wanted to summarize some important points regarding the dummy data for PK (Pharmacokinetics) and ADA (Anti-Drug Antibodies) for our study:

1) In any blinded study, only the active group will have PK and ADA samples tested. The placebo group will not have any PK and ADA samples tested.

2) Only the personnel from the BioA Lab will have access to unblinded information and will decide which samples to test. Again, the placebo group will not be tested.

3) Up until the occurrence of the study Clinical Data Lock (CDL), the study team will only have access to PK and ADA results for blinded studies.

4) Dummy data is prepared specifically for the purpose of the Dry Run. This includes both blinded and open-label studies. It should be noted that PK and ADA tests usually require more time due to their complex nature, involving methodology verification and sample QC. Therefore, it is common for these tests to not meet the timeline for the Dry Run. If real data is available within the Dry Run timeframe, it is acceptable for unblinded studies.

I hope this clarifies the important aspects of the dummy data for PK and ADA. If you have any further questions or concerns, please feel free to reach out to me.

Best regards, [Your Name]
Therefore, it is common for these data not meet the timeline for the Dry Run.   please fix this sentence for any  syntax error
